extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian won from a select group after crosswinds split the peloton early, then survived the final climbs alongside ROGLIC, Izagirre, and LANDA.
tactical_patterns: Astana drove hard to close the break but failed to capitalize when selection occurred on final climbs, tactical misfire.
MATTHEWS Michael attacked over final climb despite being sprinter, forcing climbers to respond and fragmenting chase dynamics.
MASNADA Domenico's attack created space for leader ALAPHILIPPE to sit in, Quick-Step using young rider jersey tactically.
ROGLIC finished second to ALAPHILIPPE for consecutive days, revealing consistent gap to Frenchman in punchy finishes.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: backed by teammate Masnada's attack before winning from select group, team controlling race with two jerseys
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: second to Alaphilippe again suggests tactical disadvantage in explosive finale situations despite similar climbing strength
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: attacked final climb as sprinter, signal of exceptional form beyond pure speed
rider_signals.ASTANA Team: negative: drove peloton to close break but empty-handed when race split, wasted energy without protected climber in finale
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: made front selection with brother Gorka, Movistar showing depth but not finishing power
rider_signals.KONRAD Patrick: positive: bridged to front quartet late for Bora despite teammate Buchmann being dropped, opportunistic racing
